content.claim: Entry to selective grammar-school tracks functions as a capital-intensive competition requiring families to invest financial, social, and cultural resources before formal testing occurs; elementary schools serving disadvantaged populations report not offering genuine participation opportunities to pupils.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Single-study finding not yet independently cross-validated by multiple datasets
Based on qualitative/documentary evidence of school practices rather than large-scale quantitative measurement
Findings reflect conditions at one point in time; contemporary relevance not confirmed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
